REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS M-J SF INVESTMENTS, LLC, MICHAEL CASSIDY, BRIDGET HARTY-CASSIDY, THE BANNER DEVELOPMENT COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. PLAINTIFFS' CAUSES OF ACTION, AS ALLEGED CONTAIN TWO DISTINCT CAUSES OF ACTION IN EACH. SPECIFICALLY CAUSE OF ACTION ARISING OUT OF INJURIES TO PERSON AND CAUSE OF ACTION ARISING OUT OF INJURIES TO PROPERTY. ALLSTATE INS. CO. V. MEL RAPTON, INC. (2000) 77 CAL.APP.4TH 901. DEMURRER TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION, TO THE EXTENT IT ARISES OUT OF INJURY TO PROPERTY, IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND: PLAINTIFF STATES IN OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER THAT PLAINTIFF "SEEKS ONLY DAMAGES FOR PERSONAL INJURIES." DEMURRER TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION, TO THE EXTENT THEY ARISE OUT OF PERSONAL INJURY, IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES DELAYED DISCOVERY. CONTRARY TO DEFENDANT'S READING OF THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE SAC, PLAINTIFF NEVER ALLEGES THAT HE, AS OPPOSED TO OTHERS, KNEW ABOUT THE DEFECTS IN FEBRUARY 2016 OR AT ANY TIME BEFORE JANUARY 2018. DEMURRER TO ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS IS SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY INSTRUCTED PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS. PLAINTIFF CHOSE TO NOT MAKE ANY SUBSTANTIVE AMENDMENTS TO ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS. TO ALLEGE FACTS GIVING RISE TO CONCLUSORY STATEMENTS IN SAC, E.G. FACTS GIVING RISE TO THE CONCLUSION THAT DEFENDANTS "FAILED TO OBSERVE NORMAL CORPORATE AND BUSINESS FORMALITIES" OR "FAILED TO KEEP PROPER ACCOUNTING RECORDS," ETC. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

THE REQUESTED MATERIAL IS CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE RELEVANT EVIDENCE; DISCOVERY IS BROAD UNLESS NOT DESIGNED TO LEAD TO DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE RELEVANT EVIDENCE, OR PRIVILEGED. THE SUBPOENA IS HEREBY MODIFIED TO EXCLUDE PERSONAL FINANCIAL INFORMATION THAT WAS SUBMITTED TO OBTAIN THE LOAN OR FINANCING, BASED ON THE MOVING PARTIES' PRIVACY RIGHTS. DEFENDANTS DO NOT DISPUTE PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT ENTITIES HAVE LIMITED OR NO PRIVACY RIGHTS. THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANTS' REQUEST THAT THEY SERVE AS GATEKEEPER ON THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS, GIVEN THEIR CONFLICT OF INTEREST. SANCTIONS AGAINST MOVING PARTIES AND THEIR COUNSEL OF RECORD IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION BECAUSE DEFENDANTS FAILED TO ENGAGE IN GOOD FAITH MEET AND CONFER AND MISREPRESENTED THE SUBPOENA TO THE COURT IN ITS PAPERS BY OMITTING THE IMPORTANT CAPITALIZED PORTION OF THE SUBPOENA THAT EXPLICITLY STATES, "NO FINANCIAL INFORMATION OF THE BORROWER(S) IS REQUESTED BY THIS SUBPOENA." PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER WITH SANCTION AMOUNT BLANK, IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
George W. Wolff, esq. (415-788-1881 x.222) for Plaintiff/opposing party Colby Lam.
Laura Gildengorin, esq. and Dana Andreoli, esq. (415-421-3400) of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP for Defendants/Moving party M-J SF INVESTMENTS, LLC, MICHAEL CASSIDY, BRIDGET HARTY-CASSIDY, THE BANNER DEVELOPMENT COMPANY.

Mr. Wolff stated he ordered a court reporter, who is not present, and is willing to proceed without a court reporter. Defendant submits to the tentative ruling. ARGUED re: alter-ego, overlap in share holders, legal conclusions, no factual basis or substantive changes. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs M-J SF INVESTMENTS, LLC, MICHAEL CASSIDY, BRIDGET HARTY-CASSIDY, THE BANNER DEVELOPMENT COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Plaintiffs' causes of action, as alleged contain two distinct causes of action in each. Specifically cause of action arising out of injuries to person and cause of action arising out of injuries to property. Allstate Ins. Co. v. Mel Rapton, Inc. (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 901. Demurrer to each cause of action, to the extent it arises out of injury to property, is sustained without leave to amend: Plaintiff states in opposition to the demurrer that Plaintiff "seeks only damages for personal injuries." Demurrer to all causes of action, to the extent they arise out of personal injury, is OVERRULED. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges delayed discovery. Contrary to Defendant's reading of the allegations in the SAC, Plaintiff never alleges that he, as opposed to others, knew about the defects in February 2016 or at any time before January 2018. Demurrer to alter ego allegations is sustained with final leave to amend. The Court has previously instructed Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions. Plaintiff chose to not make any substantive amendments to alter ego allegations. to allege facts giving rise to conclusory statements in SAC, e.g. facts giving rise to the conclusion that Defendants "failed to observe normal corporate and business formalities" or "failed to keep proper accounting records," etc. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

Motion To Quash Or Modify Plaintiff'S Subpoena Seeking Financial Records From First-Citizens Bank And Trust Company, Including Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
George W. Wolff, esq. (415-788-1881 x.222) for Plaintiff/opposing party Colby Lam.
Laura Gildengorin, esq. and Dana Andreoli, esq. (415-421-3400) of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP for Defendants/Moving party M-J SF INVESTMENTS, LLC, MICHAEL CASSIDY, BRIDGET HARTY-CASSIDY, THE BANNER DEVELOPMENT COMPANY.

Plaintiff accepts tentative ruling; defendant contests sanctions. ARGUED re: Meet and confer efforts. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs M-J SF INVESTMENTS, LLC, JAMES NUNEMACHER, THE BANNER DEVELOPMENT COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA, MICHAEL CASSIDY, and BRIDGET HARTY-CASSIDY's Motion To Quash Or Modify Plaintiff'S Subpoena Seeking Financial Records From First-Citizens Bank And Trust Company, Including Request For Monetary Sanctions is denied in part granted in part. The subpoena will not be quashed. The requested material is calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible relevant evidence; discovery is broad unless not designed to lead to discovery of admissible relevant evidence, or privileged. The subpoena is hereby modified to exclude personal financial information that was submitted to obtain the loan or financing, based on the moving parties' privacy rights. Defendants do not dispute plaintiff's argument that entities have limited or no privacy rights. The Court rejects defendants' request that they serve as gatekeeper on the requested documents, given their conflict of interest. Sanctions against moving parties and their counsel of record is TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION because defendants failed to engage in good faith meet and confer and misrepresented the subpoena to the Court in its papers by omitting the important capitalized portion of the subpoena that explicitly states, "NO FINANCIAL INFORMATION OF THE BORROWER(S) IS REQUESTED BY THIS SUBPOENA." Prevailing party to submit proposed order with sanction amount blank, in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
